audit_items.proof_trace: ber_strdup(pw) at L1476 returns NULL when ber_memalloc_x fails (confirmed: memory.c:643 returns p only if alloc succeeds). The result is assigned directly to passwd.bv_val with no NULL check, and strlen(passwd.bv_val) is called immediately at L1477, producing a NULL pointer dereference on OOM. No downstream guard exists — the proof of safety fails at L1477.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker or OOM condition during interactive password entry (tool invoked with -W flag) causes ber_strdup to return NULL; strlen(passwd.bv_val) at L1477 then dereferences NULL, crashing the LDAP client tool with SIGSEGV.
